GitHub Actions workflow for building Pebble firmware using QEMU on specified branches and paths.

# Purpose
The YAML configuration file defines a GitHub Actions workflow named `Build Firmware (QEMU)`. This workflow triggers on `push` and `pull_request` events to the `main` branch, specifically when changes occur in specified paths such as `.github/workflows/build-qemu.yml`, `resources/**`, and others. The workflow consists of a single job named `build`, which runs on an `ubuntu-24.04` environment using a specified Docker container image. The job employs a matrix strategy to build firmware for different board configurations: `snowy_bb2`, `spalding_bb2`, and `silk_bb2`. The steps in the job include marking the GitHub workspace as safe, checking out the repository, installing Python dependencies, configuring the build environment, building the firmware images, and storing the resulting artifacts.
